title: Testimonials — home-page vouches block (top 3)
problem: The home page needs social proof without overwhelming visitors with a wall of vouches. Testimonials shows the top 3 most recent approved vouches with a HomeDrillCard linking to /vouches for the full wall. Self-hides when no vouches are approved — keeps the home page honest for the early-practice state.
analysis: GET /api/public/vouches on mount; slice top 3 (HOME_LIMIT). Each rendered as a card with name + relationship pill + body. HomeDrillCard → /vouches at the bottom. Returns null when zero approved vouches. Feature-coloured as 'shipped' (HOME_SECTION_FEATURE['testimonials']) since vouches are the social-proof leg of the shipped feature.

decisions.decision: Top 3 only on home; full wall at /vouches
decisions.rationale: Three is enough to signal 'this is real'; more is noise on a home page. The drill-card is the affordance for visitors who want more.
decisions.recommendation: Don't change HOME_LIMIT. The number was tuned for visual density.

decisions.decision: Self-hide when zero vouches, no 'no testimonials yet' copy
decisions.rationale: Same logic as FeaturedProjects: early-practice self-hide is more honest than skeleton state copy.

successCriteria:
- Top 3 approved vouches on home
- Self-hide when empty
- HomeDrillCard routes to /vouches
- Bilingual at full parity
implementation: src/components/Testimonials.tsx — useEffect → listPublicVouches, slice top HOME_LIMIT. Each card uses relationship-pill styling shared with /vouches. Returns null when array is empty.
userGuide: Vouches appear here once moderated to approved via /admin/vouches. To change the number shown: edit HOME_LIMIT constant.
knownLimitations: Top 3 is hardcoded; not configurable per visitor / locale
No filter by relationship — newest 3 regardless of type
Self-hide hides analytics signal of 'empty practice'

statusHistory.reason: Component shipped paired with the vouches moderation flow.
Code-first doc co-located. Top-3 + self-hide stable.
